title: vangueria agrestis
intro: Vangueria agrestis, also known as the Bush Willow or Uvuma-Rhassia, is a shrub native to southern Africa that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Vangueria agrestis include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
